name: Seafood Stuffed Flounder
recipeInstructions:
1. The flounder should be between 1 to 1-1/4 pounds each, boned, heads removed, and the brown side split down the center.
2. Combine the first seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl; mix well and set aside.
3. In a large skillet, fry the bacon over high heat until crisp. Add onions, celery, and bell peppers. Stir well and saute until vegetables start to get tender, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add 3 tablespoons of butter and the white and red peppers; stir until butter is melted. Stir in the shrimp and the first seasoning mix. Continue cooking for about 3 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom well.
5. Stir in the stock and oysters; cook and stir for about 6 to 8 minutes. Remove from heat.
6. Use a slotted spoon to spoon the seafood vegetable mixture into a food processor or blender, leaving the liquid in the skillet. Process until smooth, about 15 to 30 seconds. Return the mixture to the skillet, stirring to blend with liquid; turn heat to high and cook until it starts sticking excessively, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom well.
7. Remove from heat. Meanwhile, melt the remaining butter in a saucepan over high heat. When almost melted, remove from heat, then add 1/4 cup of flour. Stir until smooth. Return to high heat for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
8. Turn down the heat under the stuffing mixture and gradually add the butter-flour mixture, stirring constantly until well blended. If it starts weeping oil, stir in about 2 tablespoons more stock or water. Continue cooking until very thick, about 1 to 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
9. Add green onions and cook for an additional minute. Remove from heat and stir in Parmesan. Cool slightly, then refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes.
10. In a small bowl, thoroughly combine the flounder seasoning mix ingredients. Open each flounder and sprinkle with half of the seasoning mix on the inside. Mound 1/4 cup cheddar cheese in the center, then spoon a scant 1/2 cup chilled stuffing on top. Close the fish so the stuffing doesn't show.
11. Cover and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours.
12. Sprinkle remaining seasoning mix on each side of each flounder, patting it in with hands.
13. In another bowl, combine the remaining seasoning mix with the flour. Heat vegetable oil over high heat in a large skillet until about 350°F.
14. Place each flounder (split side up) in the seasoned flour to coat only the bottom surface. Carefully slide into the hot oil and fry until crispy and brown, about 3 to 4 minutes without draining.
15. Transfer the flounders still split side up onto an ungreased baking sheet and bake at 550°F until cooked through and well browned on top, about 10 minutes. After 4 minutes, drape aluminum foil over the tails to prevent burning.
16. Serve immediately or with Hollandaise Sauce, Shrimp and Crab Buttercream Sauce, or Bearnaise Sauce.
